heading: Verifications involving junk and salvage motor vehicles
label: 33110 Verifications involving junk and salvage motor vehicles
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)33110(a)15:2026a(a) (2d sentence)., , , § 607; added , , .Oct. 20, 1972Oct. 25, 1992Pub. L. 92–51386 Stat. 947Pub. L. 102–519, § 306(a)106 Stat. 339733110(b)15:2026a(a) (1st, last sentences).33110(c)15:2026a(b).In subsection (b)(1)(B), the words “or derivative thereof” are omitted as unnecessary because of the definition of “vehicle identification number” in subsection (a) of the revised section.In subsection (b)(2)(A)(i), the words “has not been informed under the procedures prescribed” are substituted for “has not received a determination under” for clarity and consistency in the revised chapter. In clause (ii), the words “has not otherwise established whether” are substituted for “to otherwise determine whether” for clarity.In subsection (b)(2)(B), the words “When a carrier transfers a motor vehicle for which the carrier has not established whether the vehicle has been reported as stolen, the carrier shall provide written certification to the transferee that the carrier has not established whether the vehicle has been reported as stolen” are substituted for “except that such carrier shall provide a written certification of such lack of determination” for clarity and because of the restatement.

children.heading: .—Definition
children.label: a .—Definition
children.text: In this section, “vehicle identification number” means a unique identification number (or derivative of that number) assigned to a passenger motor vehicle by a manufacturer in compliance with applicable regulations.

children.label: A
children.text: under procedures the Attorney General prescribes by regulation under in consultation with the Secretary of Transportation, verify whether the vehicle is reported as stolen; andsection 33109 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: provide the purchaser or transferee of the vehicle from the insurance carrier verification identifying the vehicle identification number and verifying that the vehicle has not been reported as stolen or, if reported as stolen, that the carrier has recovered the vehicle and has proper legal title to the vehicle.

children.label: i
children.text: has not been informed under the procedures prescribed in that the vehicle has not been reported as stolen; orsection 33109 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: has not otherwise established whether the vehicle has been reported as stolen.

children.label: B
children.text: When a carrier transfers a motor vehicle for which the carrier has not established whether the vehicle has been reported as stolen, the carrier shall provide written certification to the transferee that the carrier has not established whether the vehicle has been reported as stolen.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: c .—Regulations
children.text: In consultation with the Secretary, the Attorney General shall prescribe regulations necessary to ensure that verification performed and provided by an insurance carrier under subsection (b)(1)(B) of this section is uniform, effective, and resistant to fraudulent use.
